title: Metastelma salinarum
taxonomy: Metastelma salinarum is classified within the Apocynaceae family, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Apocynoideae being one of the largest and most widespread. Metastelma salinarum belongs to the tribe Metastelmatinae within the subfamily Apocynoideae. The genus Metastelma is a group of flowering plants characterized by their trumpet-shaped flowers and twining or climbing growth habit. As a member of this genus, Metastelma salinarum shares these distinctive characteristics with its relatives.
uses: Metastelma salinarum, also known as the salt vine, is a species of plant with potential medicinal properties. intro: Metastelma salinarum, also known as Salin's Metastelma, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to the Mediterranean region and the Middle East. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Metastelma salinarum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
